text:
Once upon a time in the heart of darkness there lived a curious rat named Rufus.

One autumn evening Rufus ventured far from his cozy burrow out into the big city. He discovered wonderful things. He ate an apple from Granny Weatherwax’s tree—it wouldn't float away. He rode a carousel, spinning circles like a mad squirrel. He listened to jazz from birds singing through windpipes.

Days turned into weeks. Rufus fell asleep atop a newspaper during a blackout—which didn't harm him—a secret star visitor.

He visited museums every weekend, learning why cats like shiny objects. One day he climbed up inside an eagle feather exhibit—it went terribly funny. But Rufus liked funny.

That autumn passed into winter as fast as autumn. The city became icy. Rufus danced beneath an ice-blue sky. Stars fell.

A storm blew in.

And that's when he made his biggest discovery.

Something had lived here long before anything breathed life over rooftops.

They were bats.

R
